title: eryngium riparium
intro: Eryngium riparium, also known as the riparian sea holly or sea lavender, is a perennial plant species native to Europe, specifically in the coastal areas of the Mediterranean region. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can be attributed to the presence of sesquiterpenes and other bioactive compounds. Eryngium riparium has been reported to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving respiratory issues, and supporting the immune system. The plant can be used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat respiratory issues, wounds, and skin conditions.
